item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image comes from here, and the company could not be reached for permission to use this image. As such, the image is assumed not compliant and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've no idea how this works, and I don't think that's intentional (as in, "The Foundation hasn't figured it out yet"). The experiments tell me the Foundation understands how the pills and channels work to some degree, but…

Are the pills only for setting the Channel 4 person? How do you get someone to "transmit" the other channels, then? Or do the pills for each channel come from the supply on Channel 4? Then how do you know or select what Channel you are setting a person to?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Disciplinary action to follow." Why? Maybe I'm missing something… But I don't see why disciplinary action would be required? Even if it was, by putting this as the last line of the article, it takes away from the intrigue created by the rest of the O5's instructions, personally speaking.
Instead of leaving me thinking about the possible implications, it leaves me thinking about a researcher getting punished.

Still, the rest of the article was great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent idea!

I added the MC&D tag. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ending was excellent!
This is a prime SCP to use in a tale btw.
…Shouldn't this be tagged with MC&D?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The best part of this article is, by far, the implication given at the end that there are other organizations doing exactly what the Foundation is doing with these goggles. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I very much like the threat this poses, MC&D does seem like the kind of organization who would plant a mole SCP in the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, however there are some serious tone issues that until fixed I cannot in good conscious upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the fix, Mackenzie… and looks like you fixed the images too. I posted this SCP from work so at the time I wasn't able to upload the images properly but I seem to have forgotten it needed fixing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We use metric measurements on this site, not Imperial.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds good.

Also upvoted for being a good execution of seeing through another's eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A meter is about three feet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hah, that's my own silliness in being a terrible estimator. It's absolutely way too big and I think 8x8 feet is fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Think floorspace in a house. You don't give measurements for ceiling height unless it's notable. I imagine there's a default height of containment units, and unless that needs to be changed, it won't be specified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't it be an 8mX8mX8m metal containment cell?

Unless it's two-dimensional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this object, but…

SCP-1089 is to be kept in an 8x8 meter metal containment cell.

Why so much space? I think, a common secure locker or safe would be enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Set channel 4 to watch some memetic SCP and then move in on the reports of behaviour consistent with an outbreak of said SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it needs a bit more work, but I like it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rewrite complete! Thanks again for all your feedback and support.

Ultimately, I decided this didn't need to be a Factory item - I like the mass-production aspect but it's not like Factory items are the only ones mass-produced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a Factory item, there's the mass-production aspect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks everyone so much for the feedback and suggestions. I'm going to work on revising this through edit today.

Bright's post got me thinking the 'Factory' origin doesn't really add much. I think this could pretty easily stand on its own. Anyone have a compelling reason why this should stay as a 'Factory' item?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's contained in an 8x8 metal room…. 8x8 what? feet? meters? miles? gimme a unit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed two of those just now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This way too technological to be Factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great idea, starts out mundane but the final revelation is creepy.

However, to get my upvote, please fix the following section:

D-1089-1 as SCP-1089-4 was briefly observed before termination. D-1089-2, surgically blinded, was observed next. Observation of SCP-1089-4/2 was reported to be blank in a manner consistent with Channel 1. D-1089-2 was then scheduled for immediate termination so that the channel could be freed up for more useful research. D-1089-3 ingested SCP-1089-p and was placed into solitary confinement for a period of 48 hours. As it seems there are no adverse effects to SCP-1089-p other than linking the target for observation, SCP-1089/3 was reassigned to SCP-████ so that observation would yield more data than "still in solitary confinement" - SCP-1089-2 does that already.

I mean… what? Way too many numbers, and I can't figure out what the subject and object of the sentence is in a few places.
Specifically this "D-1089-1 as SCP-1089-4 was briefly observed before termination" is just nonsensical. Did you mean:
D-1089-1, like SCP-1089-4 before, was briefly observed before termination? Even that doesn't really add anything. Stick with one D-class subject at a time, maybe a bulleted list?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig it. Reminds me vaguely of the palantir in Lord of the Rings - you never know who else might be watching.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wording and tone issues:

dissipate instantly and harmlessly into the air when or is opened or pierced.

upon ingestion the compartment was suddenly empty.

link expires when the marked individual does and compartment '4' once again contained SCP-1089-p.

She has never been observed to smile

Every time as has been observed has been the same food-paste and the same plain grey dress.

has been deemed largely a waste of time.

so that observation would yield more data than "still in solitary confinement"

Otherwise pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has its good points, but the writing is a bit clumsy for me to upvote right now. I like the contents of the channels though. The O5 note didn't seem terribly earth-shattering to me, not sure what others see as the big deal with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This ended up being MUCH better than I expected… Honestly not sure why I expected so low. The slew of obliterated posts lately?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I thought this might be way too useful to be good, but the O5 order really makes the whole article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the focus put on the seperate channels, leaving a lot up to the reader to imagine what MC&D was doing to those people, or what groups may have had it before in order to observe the other high-value individuals. Plus, I like to think the woman who murdered the Japanese businessman was a cannibal.

Anyway, point is I see this as a wonderful example of working past just "thing with anomalous property." The story really does make a good deal of an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…we've given them crystal clear surveillance of the inside of our secure facility as well as SCP-████!"

That caught me off guard. Very well written, excellent little bit of suspense at the end. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. I like this one a lot. The beginning could use a tiny bit of editing to make it more clinical, but regardless, this one is great. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just wanted to give special thanks to Mackenzie and Quikngruvn for help refining this one in chat!
